questions.title: First Heading
questions.question:
Create a web page that displays the text **"Hello, World!"** as the main heading of the page.

Use the appropriate HTML element for a top-level heading.
questions.answer: Edit `index.html` so that the page contains a single top-level heading with the text `Hello, World!`.

startingFiles.html:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <head>
    <title>My First Page</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <!-- Write your heading here -->
  </body>
</html>
startingFiles.css: /* No CSS needed for this question */

questions.hints:
- The `

` element is used for the most important heading on a page. - The text goes between the opening `

` and closing `

` tags. - Make sure the text matches exactly, including the comma and exclamation mark.
questions.walkthrough:
1. Open `index.html`. 2. Find the `` section — this is where visible content goes. 3. Inside ``, add an `

` element: - Pseudocode: `

` + your text + `

` 4. Put the exact text `Hello, World!` between the tags. 5. Save and refresh the preview. You should see large bold text. 6. Run the tests to confirm the `

` exists and has the correct text.

questions.title: Paragraphs
questions.question:
Add two paragraphs of text to the page below the existing heading.

The first paragraph should say: `I am learning HTML.`
The second paragraph should say: `It is fun!`
questions.answer:
Add two `

` elements inside the `` of `index.html`, each containing the required text.

startingFiles.html: About Me

questions.hints:
- Paragraphs use the `

` tag. - Each paragraph should be its own `

...

` element. - Place them **after** the `

` but still **inside** ``.
questions.solution:
```html About Me

About Me

I am learning HTML.

It is fun!

```
questions.walkthrough:
1. Locate the comment in `` that says where to add your paragraphs. 2. Replace the comment with your first paragraph element: - Pseudocode: `

` + text + `

` 3. On the next line, add the second paragraph the same way. 4. Double-check the text is exact — the tests compare character by character. 5. Save, refresh the preview, and run the tests.

questions.title: Links And Images
questions.question:
Add the following to the page:

1. A link that says `Visit Example` and goes to `https://example.com`.
2. An image with the source `https://via.placeholder.com/150` and the alt text `Placeholder image`.
questions.answer: Use the `` element for the link (with an `href` attribute) and the `` element for the image (with `src` and `alt` attributes).

startingFiles.html: Links and Images

questions.hints: - `link text` creates a clickable link. - `` is a self-closing element — it has no closing tag. - The `alt` attribute is required for accessibility.
questions.solution:
```html Links and Images

Links and Images

Visit Example ```
questions.walkthrough: 1. Inside ``, after the heading, add an anchor element: - Pseudocode: `...` - Set `href` to `https://example.com` and the inner text to `Visit Example`. 2. Below the link, add the image: - Pseudocode: `` - Set `src` to the placeholder URL and `alt` to `Placeholder image`. 3. Refresh the preview — you should see the text link and the image. 4. Run the tests to confirm the attributes match.

questions.title: Unordered List
questions.question:
Create an unordered list of your three favorite fruits. The list items, in order, must be:

1. Apple
2. Banana
3. Cherry
questions.answer: Use a `<ul>` element with three `<li>` children inside `<body>`.

startingFiles.html: My Favorite Fruits

questions.hints:
- `<ul>` creates an unordered (bulleted) list.
- Each item inside the list uses `<li>`.
- Keep the order the same as the question.
questions.walkthrough:
1. After the `

`, open a `
    ` tag. 2. Inside the `
      `, add three `
    • ` elements — one per fruit. - Pseudocode: ```
      • fruit 1
      • fruit 2
      • fruit 3
      ``` 3. Close the `
    `. 4. Verify in the preview that you see three bulleted items in the correct order.

questions.title: Text Color
questions.question:
Make the `<h1>` heading on the page display in **red**.

Use CSS to style the heading — do not use inline `style` attributes in the HTML.
questions.answer: In `style.css`, add a rule that targets `h1` and sets `color` to `red`.

startingFiles.html: I should be red

questions.hints:
- CSS rules look like: `selector { property: value; }`
- The selector for an `<h1>` element is simply `h1`.
- The property for text color is `color`.
questions.walkthrough:
1. Open `style.css`.
2. Write a selector that targets the heading element:
   - Pseudocode: `h1 { ... }`
3. Inside the braces, set the `color` property to `red`.
   - Pseudocode: `color: red;`
4. Save and refresh the preview — the heading should now appear red.
5. Run the tests. The computed color `rgb(255, 0, 0)` is the same as `red`.

questions.title: Background And Font Size
questions.question:
Style the page so that:

1. The page background is light yellow (`#ffffcc`).
2. All paragraphs have a font size of exactly `20px`.
questions.answer: In `style.css`, target the `body` element for the background color and the `p` element for the font size.

startingFiles.html:
Styling Basics

This paragraph should be 20 pixels tall.

So should this one.

questions.hints:
- `background-color` sets the background of an element.
- Hex color `#ffffcc` equals `rgb(255, 255, 204)`.
- `font-size` takes a length value like `20px`.
questions.walkthrough:
1. Open `style.css`.
2. Add a rule for the `body` selector and set `background-color` to `#ffffcc`.
3. Add a second rule for the `p` selector and set `font-size` to `20px`.
   - Pseudocode:
     ```
     body { background-color: <color>; }
     p    { font-size: <size>; }
     ```
4. Save and refresh — you should see a pale yellow background and slightly larger paragraph text.
5. Run the tests to verify both the color and font size.

questions.title: Class Selector
questions.question:
The HTML contains two paragraphs. Only the one with the class `highlight` should:

1. Have a yellow background (`yellow`).
2. Be displayed in bold.

The other paragraph must remain unstyled.
questions.answer: In `style.css`, use a class selector (`.highlight`) to apply `background-color: yellow` and `font-weight: bold`.

startingFiles.html:
This paragraph should look normal.

This paragraph should be highlighted.

questions.hints:
- A class selector in CSS starts with a dot, e.g. `.myClass`.
- Only elements with `class="myClass"` will match.
- Use `font-weight: bold;` to make text bold.
questions.walkthrough:
1. Look at the HTML — only the second `

` has `class="highlight"`. 2. In `style.css`, write a class selector: - Pseudocode: `.highlight { ... }` 3. Inside the block, set `background-color: yellow;` and `font-weight: bold;`. 4. Because the first paragraph has no class, it will not match this selector and stays unstyled. 5. Refresh the preview and run the tests.

questions.title: Center Text
questions.question: Center-align the text of the `<h1>` heading horizontally using CSS.

startingFiles.html:
Center me

This paragraph can stay on the left.

questions.hints:
- The `text-align` property controls horizontal alignment of text inside a block element.
- Valid values include `left`, `right`, `center`, and `justify`.
- Only style the `h1`, not the `body` — otherwise the paragraph will also move.
questions.walkthrough:
1. Open `style.css`.
2. Add a rule for the `h1` selector:
   - Pseudocode: `h1 { text-align: center; }`
3. Save and refresh the preview. The heading should now appear horizontally centered, while the paragraph stays left-aligned.
4. Run the tests to confirm the computed `text-align` is `center`.
